Not all mutations occur in coding sequence!

Examples of Nonsense Mutations
ONLY in coding sequence
5’TAC3’ = Tyr
5’TAG3’ = stop
5’TGC3’ = Cys
5’TGA3’ = stop
5’TCG3’ = Ser
5’TAG3’ = stop
These are examples, not a complete list. The stop codons are:
5’TAA3’
5’TAG3’
5’TGA3’
Question: how many different amino acids have codons that can be
mutated into a stop codon by a single nucleotide change?

Insertion in coding sequence = Frameshift Mutation

Thymine dimer formation
also caused by peroxides, free radicals, etc

Thymine dimers stress backbone, distort double helix
“kink” in the DNA, helix bends

5’__________3’
C   G    A    T   ??
DNA polymerase will stall at thymine dimer
eventually either leave gap or put in something (usually 3 As)

Repair = remove and replace damaged DNA
Reversal = second mutation that reverses the original mutation
restores original DNA sequence
Reversion = second mutation, reverses the EFFECT of the initial mutation
does NOT change initial mutation
Intragenic reversion – second mutation, same gene
Intergenic reversion – second mutation in a different gene

Intragenic reversion
example based on
amino acid charge
direct reversal
second mutation
same site
second mutation
different site in
same gene

Intergenic reversion

5’UUC   CAG  UAC  AAA  GAG3’      wildtype mRNA, transcribed from wildtype DNA
3’AAG5’ GUC  AUG UUU  CUC       tRNA anticodons
phe     gln    tyr    lys      ala        amino acid added
5’UUC   CAG  UAA  AAA  GAG3’      mutant mRNA, transcribed from mutant DNA
3’AAG5’ GUC  stop 			    premature termination due to nonsense mutation
phe     gln				    mutant, truncated protein
5’UUC   CAG  UAA  AAA  GAG3’    mutant mRNA, transcribed from mutant DNA
3’AAG5’ GUC  AUU UUU  CUC      mutant glu-tRNA = suppressor tRNA
phe     gln    glu    lys      ala        protein with only 1 amino acid change

5’UUC   CAG  UAA  AAA  GAG3’	 mutant mRNA, transcribed from mutant DNA
3’AAG5’ GUC  AUU UUU  CUC     	 mutant glu-tRNA = suppressor tRNA
phe     gln    glu    lys      ala        	protein with only 1 amino acid change
glu codon =			     	  	 5’GAA3’
normal glu tRNA anticodon =		 3’CUU5’
mutant glu-tRNA anticodon =		 3’AUU5’
complementary to stop codon = 	 5’UAA3’
Suppressor tRNA = any mutant tRNA whose anticodon is complementary
to any one of the stop codons
